In the automotive space we've streamlined the licensing of over 200 million connected vehicles to date (https://www.avanci.com/vehicle/) and are continuously expanding into new verticals. **Essential Duties and Responsibilities** * Gather, document, and validate business requirements through interviews, workshops, and data analysis * Evaluate current processes and systems to identify gaps, inefficiencies, and improvement opportunities in the current financial collections and distribution applications * Develop process maps, workflows, and documentation to support solution design * Partner with technical teams and internal stakeholders to ensure requirements are understood and implemented effectively * Define requirements for system improvements or new solutions, including customer\-facing interfaces * Lead end‑to‑end project planning, execution, monitoring, and delivery * Define project scope, timelines, milestones, and resource needs * Track progress, manage risks, and escalate issues when needed * Ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with business goal **Required Skills, Professional, and Technical Expertise** * Ability to understand business needs of key stakeholders * Ability to work independently * Strong analytical and organizational skills * Proactive and structured in problem solving * Proficient in data visualization * Familiarity with project management methodologies (Agile, Scrum, Waterfall) * Up\-to\-date knowledge of technology applications including: + MS Excel (Expert level proficiency, incl. VBA, Macros etc.) + CRM systems (experience with HubSpot is a plus) + Database management tools and software, incl SQL + Web portal design or administration **Education and Experience** * Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field * Minimum 5 years work experience * Experience in leading systems implementation projects from requirements to delivery This is an opportunity to join a pioneering company at the forefront of global technology licensing. You will work with an international team helping support platforms that enable innovation across industries.

title: Candidate CNS/CNS Hepatobiliary (HPB) - HR26031
description: Job Details **Reference:** HR26031 **Job Nature:** Full Time Permanent **Job Location:** St Vincent's University Hospital **Grade:** Clinical Nurse Specialist Candidate (General) **Overview** Applications are invited for the position of Candidate CNS/CNS in the OPAT Department of St. Vincent’s University Hospital. Informal Enquiries or Role Specific Enquiries to: Mr Karl Pratt O’Brien, Directorate Nurse Manager, K.PrattOBrien@st\-vincents.ie **Please download the candidate information booklet for this position, as this will not be available online after the vacancy close date.** Applications for St. Vincent’s University Hospital job vacancies will only be accepted through SVUH Careers Page. Any applications submitted after the vacancy has closed **will not be accepted.** You will receive a confirmation email within 2 working days that your application has been received. We highly recommend that you contact us by emailing recruitment@svuh.ie if you do not hear from us. Closing date of applications: Friday 1st May Key Responsibilities The post holder’s practice is based on the five core concepts of Clinical Nurse Specialist HPB role as defined by the NCNM 4th edition (2008\) in order to fulfil the role. The concepts are: * Comply with NMBI, An Bord Altranais Code of Professional Conduct and Scope of Practice for Nursing. * Contribute to the holistic assessment, planning, implementation and evaluation of quality nursing care to patients requiring urological cancer services, in order to increase health gain and quality of life. * Provide specialist knowledge, expertise and care in liaison with the multidisciplinary team to meet the needs of patients and their families in implementing a care plan thereby ensuring a cohesive and effective service is provided. * Provide nursing expertise for patients using the service that optimises quality and continuity of care between primary and secondary care. * Ensure patient confidentially is respected and the dignity of the patient is assured and maintained at all times having regard to the Philosophy and Ethical code of the hospital. * Provide a safe environment for patients/ other staff/ self, with due regard to Health, Safety and Risk Management issues. * Assist in meeting the psychological, emotional and informational needs of patients and their families at the time of diagnosis and throughout treatment. * Be a role model demonstrating high standards of clinical practice and provide clinical leadership in relevant specialist area. * Maintain awareness of current developments in the treatment and management of HPB cancer patients. Act as an effective role model for other staff, administering the service through evidence based practice and patient care initiatives, aimed at improving quality of care. * Identify and utilise professional and lay resources and facilities at local and national level by direct or indirect referral. * Act as patient advocate within the multidisciplinary team. Identify the educational needs of the patients and ensure they have access to relevant information enabling an understanding of their disease and available treatment options. * Communicate, negotiate and represent patient’s values and decisions in collaboration with other professionals and family members. * Contribute to the development of the hospitals philosophy, which advocates the privacy of the patient in all its activities and in developing an environment, which supports the value of nursing care. * To be aware of own needs regarding supervision and professional development and to work actively towards meeting these by attending appropriate workshops, seminars and training to ensure professional competency * Implement Professional Development Plans in line with the Nursing Department and Hospital Policy. * Provide information and update sessions at ward level and department level to aid staff and patients in their knowledge and understanding of Cancer Care services and keep updated with current changes in treatment. * Develop a network of contacts and communication links with other professionals in the specified field. * Be actively aware of current procedures, policies and standards in relation to Hepatobiliary and pancreatic cancer services and contribute where appropriate in formulating or updating policies and procedures that comply with agreed best practice. * Facilitate, develop and monitor quality initiatives in line with local and national requirements or guidelines. * Be actively involved in the teaching of new staff and post graduate students, in conjunction with relevant personnel. * Demonstrate leadership ability and good communication skills. * Help to foster a supportive working environment. * Be aware of all hospital policies and procedures, and collaborate with other health care professionals to ensure that these are observed. * Demonstrate ability to organise own work load effectively. * Establish clear referral patterns to communicate with members of the multidisciplinary team and other relevant professionals (e.g. General Practitioners, Public Health and Practice Nurses) and set out practices aimed at improving patient outcomes. * In all aspects of work demonstrate an ability to work across organisational and professional boundaries. * Contribute to the planning and organisation of out\-patient clinics with the multidisciplinary team. * Participate in audit of clinical care in order to monitor the effectiveness of current therapeutic regimes and to improve health outcomes. * Promote and identify areas within the speciality relevant to nursing practice, which would benefit from research with regular literature review thereby ensuring practice is evidence based. * Evaluate effectiveness of Quality initiatives undertaken and where necessary make recommendations to improve patient care. * Assist in the collection and correlation of statistical information and data in order to audit the services and aid the planning of future patient service initiatives/improvements. * Be an informed resource in the area of Cancer Nursing for nurses, doctors and other allied health professionals, promoting collaborative working and practices in all aspects of care relating to the service. * Be aware of budgetary constraints and cost implications * To develop nurse led triage service in order to see and, on occasions, treat patients within agreed protocols. * Establish and develop nurse led patient advisory clinics and make variations in prescribed clinical options within agreed dose parameters and an agreed policy framework * Provide specialist support services for patients according to speciality and need, such as a telephone advice service/specialist drop in/ outpatients’ service. * To assist ward based staff with discharge planning arrangements aimed at improving quality of life for patients in the home setting. * Where clinical trials take place, actively contribute to their effectiveness particularly in relation to informed consent and advocacy. * Ensure there is a system of documentation that reflects advanced nursing practice and ensure that staff in clinical areas maintain effective nursing records on patients receiving treatment. Evaluate these records periodically in the interest of safe practice and in line with hospital policy on record keeping. * Act as a resource nurse expert advisor for patients requiring Cancer services and attend managerial and clinical meetings that require specialist\-nursing input. * In partnership with the HPB Consultants ensure the most effective and efficient use of developments in information technology for both patient care and administrative support in a manner, which integrates well with systems throughout the organisation. * In conjunction with the relevant Consultant(s), devise and update written protocols for patients requiring this treatment aimed at maximising quality of patient care. Where necessary make recommendations to improve service. Our Requirements For Further information, please see candidate booklet. What We Offer **Benefits Of Working At St Vincent’s University Hospital** St. Vincent’s University Hospital is a recognised *Employer of Choice* in Ireland and was the first public hospital to be awarded *Joint Commission International Accreditation* in February 2010\. The range of benefits of working in the hospital includes: * Competitive salary and increments * Defined benefit pension scheme * Paid maternity leave scheme * Access to excellent learning and development opportunities * Support, including leave and/or financial, is available to employees for approved courses * Onsite and online library facilities * Subsidised staff restaurant * Various flexible working hours options available in many areas * Time off and leave options to promote work life balance and family friendly working arrangements * Subsidised pharmacy \- employees may purchase discounted medication from the Pharmacy on production of a prescription resulting in significant savings. * Generous sick pay scheme and Occupational Health Services to support attendance including free flu vaccination for employees * Access to subsidised gym facilities * Access to health services credit union * Group discount on health insurance * Free membership of Groupschemes.com which provide hundreds of discounts to members of the scheme and direct access to a wide range of savings on day\-to\-day items across a wide range of products and services including travel, clothing, motor, health and wellbeing, music and entertainment. * Tax saver commuter ticket scheme \- employees can save up to 48% of travel costs as a result of tax savings * Cycle to Work Scheme and changing facilities * Access to Health Promotion services including smoking cessation programme * Active Social Committee * Excellent access to public transport including dart and bus routes. Candidate Information Booklet This booklet contains all the information candidates need to know about the role. View PDF

title: Capital Allowances Consultant - Quantity Surveyor
description: When you join KPMG Ireland, you are joining a community of over 4,200 employees who collectively work together, both in person and virtually, to achieve their full potential. We are known as an organisation who want you to come as you are and do work that truly matters. Joining KPMG Ireland is an opportunity to enhance your career, not only in the work that you enjoy doing, but equally through the global connections that you make, the lifelong learning that you can accomplish and the chance to make your mark. Are you our next **Capital Allowances Consultant \- Quantity Surveyor?** **Location: Dublin, as part of our hybrid working model.** **Grade: Consultant** **Contract type: FTC – 12 Month (Initial) Contract** This individual will have an immediate opportunity to execute an existing pipeline of work, across a range of industry sectors, comprising both: Expenditure claims / analysis, where clients have undertaken construction works directly and Purchase price allocations, where newly developed or 2nd hand buildings are acquired by clients, requiring reconstruction cost estimates. You will interact with client teams across Finance, Tax, Real Estate and Facilities functions as well as external design teams, to collate the required project cost documentation and to address queries as they arise, attending client meeting and undertaking site visits as required to identify qualifying assets and to substantiate claims. This a fantastic opportunity for an individual to step change their career with us. **A Snapshot Of Your Peer Group** * BSc Degree in Quantity Surveying, minimum 2:1 or acceptable equivalent * Minium 2\-3 years post graduate experience with a professional quantity surveying practice (construction cost consultancy) or established main contractor * An ability to prepare and price (based on recent practical experience) budget reconstruction estimates for a range of buildings (e.g., apartment blocks, commercial offices/fitout, hotels, and nursing homes etc.) * Experience in procuring and managing costs on both base build and fit out construction projects from inception to completion * Experience using Cubit, CostX or similar * Excellent MS Excel skills * Excellent communication skills, exceptional oral and written communication capabilities **Your responsibilities and achievements will evolve as you enhance your career with us. Here is what you can initially expect:** **Reconstruction Cost Estimates** * Independently preparing detailed and robust reconstruction estimates based on limited information (typically using reputable price books such as SPONS and benchmarked to published indicative construction costs (€/m2\) for the Irish market) * The reconstruction estimates typically include significant mechanical, electrical and lift packages **Construction Cost Analysis** * Liaising with project design teams (primarily client QS firm) to procure all relevant source cost documentation required and to get an understanding of the capital project * Analysing and reconciling construction cost documentation including the following: * Main Contract Bills of Quantities and Pricing Documents * Reconciliation of the tender cost documents to the Contract Sum * Specialist Subcontract Packages (typically mechanical and electrical installations) * Variations, PC Sums and Provisional Sums * Final Account Reports **Your Team** You will be joining a multidisciplinary team of Chartered Quantity Surveyors, Accountants and Tax Professionals. KPMG’s Capital Allowances and Tax Depreciation group is recognised as Ireland’s leading advisers on capital allowances. We are the only Big 4 firm in Ireland to have a dedicated multidisciplinary capital allowances team, and we are partner lead. Currently there are three Chartered Quantity Surveyors on the team and there is a steady pipeline of work to be delivered to our clients. For further information please visit the capital allowances section of our website via the link posted below: Capital allowances explained \- Deductible expenses **Discover How KPMG Can Enrich Your Life Beyond Your Career** **Benefits Tailored For You** At KPMG, we understand that life is multifaceted. That’s why we offer a flexible benefits package designed to support your unique lifestyle. Our benefits include: * Discounted Health Insurance: Prioritise your well\-being with our health insurance options. * Additional Annual Leave: Purchase extra leave to spend more time with friends and family or to travel. * Enhanced Family Leave: Enjoy more time with your family with our generous leave options. * Emergency Childcare Cover: For times when your regular childcare plans fall through * Social Events: Connect and network with colleagues through our engaging social events. * Early Finish Fridays: Start your weekends early on bank holidays and during the summer. * Relocation Assistance: Smooth your transition if you’re moving to Ireland. * Learning \& Development: Access our extensive supports, including LinkedIn Learning, to grow your skills. We also offer a variety of flexible working options to help you balance your professional and personal life. **Career Progression with Continuous Support** From the moment you join KPMG, you’ll receive dedicated support from your performance manager to enhance your skills. Our award\-winning Business School provides continuous learning opportunities to deepen your expertise. We stand out as the only Big Four firm to announce promotions four times a year, offering you frequent opportunities for career advancement. **Commitment to Inclusion \& Diversity** Inclusion, diversity, and equity are at the heart of KPMG. We celebrate diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and we’re committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable environment at every level. Our goal is to attract and retain the best talent by building a culture where everyone can achieve their potential. Learn more about our commitment here \- https://kpmg.com/ie/en/home/about/inclusion\-diversity\-equity.html **Ready to Join Us?** If you meet most of the criteria, we encourage you to apply by submitting your CV. KPMG is an equal opportunities employer, welcoming applications from people of all backgrounds. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process if needed. Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. For any specific queries about the role, please contact Sharon Griffin. For a glimpse into life at KPMG, follow \#ExperienceKPMG on LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and X. **Please note that agency assistance is not required for this position.** By clicking apply to this role your application will not only be considered for this position, but your details will be stored on our database and may be considered for similar future positions that may arise. This means that the personal data contained in your application and provided to KPMG as part of the application process may be retained by us for up to 18 months from the date of your application and you hereby agree to that. If you do not wish your details to be stored on our recruitment database, please email careers@kpmg.ie stating this and we will ensure that such information is not stored other than for the purposes of this application.

title: Care Assistant
description: Cheeverstown is a HSE\-funded Section 38 organization dedicated to empowering individuals with intellectual disabilities to lead full and meaningful lives. We provide person\-centered services tailored to individual needs, working closely with their families and support networks. Guided by our core values—Rights, Person\-Centered Care, Inclusion, and Partnership—we strive to foster dignity, respect, and full societal participation for every individual we support. Join our team in making a meaningful impact in the lives of others. **Role Description** The Care Assistant will provide compassionate, person\-centered support to individuals with intellectual disabilities, ensuring that their daily care needs are met and their independence is encouraged. Responsibilities include assisting with personal care, supporting day\-to\-day activities, fostering meaningful relationships, and promoting a safe and respectful environment. The role also involves working collaboratively with families and other team members to deliver the best possible care. We have full time and part time positions available on permanent and specified purpose contracts. **Requirements:** * A FETAC/QQI Level 5 Major Award in Social Care/ Healthcare or relevant qualification\- all 8 modules completed. * Be knowledgeable of Health Act 2007 and have experience and understanding HIQA Regulations * Values that correspond with Cheeverstown’s values. * Possess a full clean drivers license and willingness to drive * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including good listening skills. * A person\-centred approach to the provision of supports, including an understanding of the individual needs and abilities of each person. * Flexibility to work in all areas of Resident Services. * Openness to providing supports across a variety of activities. * Commitment and dedication to enhancing the lives of people with intellectual disability and autism. * Good IT and administrative skills. **Desirable:** * One years’ experience working in the area of disability is preferable. * Experience supporting people with autism.. **Please note:** All job offers are subject to two successful references, ongoing Garda Vetting, completion of mandatory training (online \& onsite) and a satisfactory pre\-employment medical. Salary will be paid at the Care Assistant grade on the HSE Consolidated Salary Scales, depending on qualifications and experience.

title: Chef de Partie
description: We are seeking a dedicated and passionate **Chef de Partie** to join our award\-winning culinary team. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing a specific section of the kitchen, ensuring the highest standards of food preparation and presentation are maintained at all times. **Key Responsibilities** * Prepare, cook, and present high\-quality dishes within your assigned section. * Assist the Sous Chef and Head Chef in menu development and daily specials. * Supervise and train junior kitchen staff (Commis Chefs and Kitchen Porters). * Maintain strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (HACCP). * Manage stock control, minimize waste, and ensure correct storage of ingredients. * Ensure consistency in portion sizes and plating standards. **Requirements \& Qualifications** * **Experience:** A minimum of 2–3 years of experience in a high\-volume, professional kitchen environment. * **Skills:** Proven ability to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment with excellent attention to detail. * **Knowledge:** Strong understanding of various cooking methods, ingredients, and European/International cuisines. * **Education:** Relevant culinary qualification is preferred. * **Communication:** Fluent English is essential for kitchen safety and coordination. **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Meals on duty. * Uniform provided. * Opportunities for career progression and training. Pay: €37,000\.00\-€38,000\.00 per year Education: * Advanced/Higher Certificate (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chief Executive Officer
company: St John of God Research Foundation
description: St John of God Research Foundation clg provides a research infrastructure and develops a culture of inquiry across the St John of God Group (SJOG Group). The Research Foundation is uniquely placed as a research company embedded within health services. As the key enabler of research across the SJOG Group, the Research Foundation is the gatekeeper of research standards across the organisation, and the point of contact for all staff and external researchers who wish to engage in research. The CEO of the SJOG Research Foundation provides strategic, operational, and values‑led leadership to advance the mission of improving the lives of people experiencing mental health difficulties, intellectual disability, and dementia through high‑quality, impactful research. The CEO is responsible for delivering the Research Foundation’s strategic plan, strengthening research governance and ethics, and ensuring excellence across all programmes, partnerships, and funded activities. The CEO leads the development of a Research Excellence Framework, oversees implementation of the PPI Strategy, and drives organisational learning, inclusion, and evidence‑based practice. The CEO builds strong relationships with academic partners, funders, government bodies, and sector organisations, positioning the Research Foundation as a national leader in research impact and collaboration. The CEO ensures robust governance, financial stewardship, and compliance with all statutory and regulatory requirements. They foster a positive, compassionate, and high‑performing culture, support staff development, and champion co‑production and lived experience involvement across all research activities. Reporting to the Board of Directors, the CEO provides clear, timely, and strategic advice to guide decision‑making and ensure the Research Foundation delivers measurable value and meaningful outcomes **.** **Leadership** · Lead the Research Foundation as the singular research pathway for all research conducted across SJOG Services. · Provide strategic leadership to deliver the Research Foundation’s mission to advance research excellence in mental health, intellectual disability, and dementia care across Ireland, the UK, and Malawi. · Ensure that all activities are in accordance with the philosophy, ethos, and values of HSG and the policies established by its Board. · Guide the implementation of the Research Foundation’s Strategy with a focus on maximising the impact of research. Report to the Board on progress against the strategy on a quarterly basis. · Provide expert leadership, guidance, and performance development to the Research Foundation team and wider staff, fostering a culture of learning, inclusion, compassion, and evidence‑based practice. · Mentor staff at all levels, promoting CPD, research capacity building, and a values‑led organisational culture. · Represent the Research Foundation nationally and internationally with stakeholders, funders, academic partners, and the public. **Strategic** · Lead and report to the Board on the implementation of the Foundation’s strategic plan, ensuring alignment with organisational values and sector priorities. · Prepare submissions, position papers, and evidence‑informed contributions to national policy, research funding calls, and sectoral initiatives. · Analyse and advise on the impact of new research opportunities, partnerships, and regulatory changes. · Develop and lead a robust evaluation framework to demonstrate the impact, quality, and value of the work of the Research Foundation. · Ensure the Research Foundation is a thought leader in research governance, PPI, inclusion, and values‑based practice. **Organisational** · Maintain strong relationships with the Board, providing timely, accurate, and strategic information to support decision‑making. · Provide oversight and support to the Research Support Service, the Library \& Information Service, PPI function, and Psychosis Research Centre. · Chair the Research Advisory Committee and ensure streamlined and appropriately governed research pathways. · Oversee the SJOG Research Grant Scheme, ensuring the integrity of the process and allocation of funding. · Work in partnership with the Research Ethics Committee in safeguarding the reputation and values of St John of God and assuring ethical research practices. · Embed co‑production and lived experience involvement across all research initiatives, governance structures, and evaluation processes. · Foster an organisational ethos that recognises and celebrates the contribution of peer researchers, volunteers, and lived experience partners. **Governance** · Design and implement operational policies and procedures to support good research governance, ensuring these procedures are compliant with data protection and health research legislation, and are in accordance with the philosophy, ethos and values of HSG. · Ensure compliance with all statutory reporting requirements (Companies Office, Charities Regulator, Lobbying Register). · Ensure delivery of contractual commitments to funders, partners, and collaborators. · Lead operational and fiscal planning, ensuring effective performance, risk management, and internal controls. · Ensure appropriate internal audit processes are in place. · Promote a positive, inclusive, and psychologically safe workplace environment. · Undertake additional projects as required by the Board. **Finance** · Provide support to the Research Grants Committee in the planning and oversight of the annual research grant scheme. · Provide accurate analysis of budgets, financial trends, and operational performance to support Board and Executive decision‑making. · Work closely with the HSG Group Director of Finance to optimise financial stewardship and strategic financial planning. · Develop cash‑flow projections, budgets, and reporting mechanisms to meet operational and strategic needs. · Work with funders, donors, and partners to maximise value for money and investment in the Research Foundation’s research programmes. · Maintain best‑practice standards in accounting, audit, and financial governance. **Stakeholder Management** · Contribute to the senior leadership of the SJOG organisation by participating in the CEO Group, the HSG Executive team, and working closely with Boards of Directors. · Represent the Research Foundation publicly, enhancing visibility and awareness, and demonstrating impact. · Build and maintain strong relationships with NGOs, research networks, universities, and sectoral collaborators. · Establish and maintain relationships with key stakeholders including public representatives, policy makers, media, and external agencies. · Promote the Research Foundation as a leader in research excellence, inclusion, and values‑based practice. **Essential Criteria** · Qualifications: PhD or relevant third‑level qualification · Minimum 3 years’ senior management experience in a research, NGO, health, or social care setting. · Minimum 3 years’ experience in financial governance and compliance. · Significant experience research management. · Strong understanding of the Charities Governance Code, governance obligations, and statutory reporting. · Demonstrated senior leadership experience with a coaching/mentoring approach. · Excellent analytical, written, verbal, and ICT skills. · Knowledge of national and international research, policy, and sector developments. · Proven track record of successful research grant funding applications at a national and international level. · Excellent communication skills across all organisational levels and with external stakeholders. · Experience working with external funders, partners, and collaborators. · Personal integrity and understanding of the reputational responsibilities of a CEO. · Full clean driving licence. **Desirable Criteria** · Experience working with a Board of Directors. · Experience in mental health, intellectual disability, social care research, or related fields, will be an advantage. · Experience engaging with Government Departments, policy makers, and funders. · Knowledge of collaborating with fundraising teams and philanthropic groups to generate funds for research. · Flexibility to work occasional unsocial hours. · Independent, creative, and innovative thinker. · Experience representing an organisation in national media or public forums. **Other Requirements** · Garda vetting · Appropriate references **Contract \& Salary** · €75k\-85k · Permanent · 6‑month probation period · 35 hours per week (exclusive of lunch) title: Chief Product & Technology Officer
description: **Chief Product \& Technology Officer** Dublin · Executive · Full\-time · Hybrid · HealthTech / SaaS A well\-established Irish healthtech business with a market\-leading position, a proprietary system\- SaaS platform, and an ambitious growth agenda across Ireland and the UK. A senior executive role reporting to the Managing Director. You'll own product and technology end\-to\-end — strategy, roadmap, platform, and team — with a seat at the leadership table and real autonomy to shape the company's next chapter. Responsibilities 1. Set and own the product and technology strategy 2. Lead development of the cloud platform, billing engine, and mobile apps 3. Build and lead the engineering and product teams 4. Drive the roadmap from discovery to delivery 5. Integrate AI and automation to improve efficiency 6. Uphold GDPR compliance and platform security Essential 1. Over 10 years senior product or technology leadership in Fintech/Healthtech/MedTech. 2. Delivered products to market – customer focussed CX journey 3. Team building and engineering leadership 4. Continuous improvement – Process Improvements\- Operational efficiencies 5. Commercially minded with product led revenue growth 6. Strong IT background in LAMP, AI and digital solutions, and Azure ( scaling cloud SaaS platforms) 7. Regulated / compliance environments

title: Civil Engineer
description: A well\-established, Irish\-owned engineering consultancy in Dublin City is seeking a **Civil Engineer with 3–6 years’ experience** to join its growing team. This organisation has built a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality infrastructure projects and offers an excellent working environment, strong company culture, and clear career progression opportunities. **The Role** Reporting to a Team Leader, the successful candidate will be involved in the design and delivery of civil engineering projects across a variety of sectors. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design of drainage, roads, levels, watermains, and earthworks * Development of innovative and cost\-effective engineering solutions * Application of relevant standards including GDSDS, IW CoP, DMURS, DMRB, and TII Publications * Coordination with multidisciplinary teams and project stakeholders * Site visits and inspections to monitor progress and quality * Responding to contractor queries and ensuring compliance with specifications * Assisting in the preparation of contract documents and administration of Public Works Contracts * Mentoring and supporting junior engineers **Candidate Requirements** * 3–6 years’ experience in civil engineering * Level 8 Degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Working towards Chartered status with Engineers Ireland (or equivalent) * Strong technical, analytical, and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Experience with relevant design software and standards * Full driver’s licence (desirable) **Benefits** * Salary range: **€45,000 – €60,000** * Pension contribution matched up to 5% * 23 days annual leave \+ additional service\-related leave * Hybrid working arrangements * Payment of professional membership subscriptions * Structured training and career development programmes * Employee wellbeing supports and EAP * Inclusive and supportive working culture * Active social committee and regular company events This is an excellent opportunity to join a respected Irish consultancy offering strong project exposure, career progression, and a positive working environment.

title: Client Relationship On-site Representative
description: **Office based role Mon to Fri \- Sandyford, Dublin.** Are you passionate about cutting\-edge financial / payments technology? Do you thrive in a fast\-paced pioneering environment where you bring you know how of introducing technology solutions to the retail and hospitality verticals within Ireland? **About Viva.com** Viva.com is the 1st Tech Bank in Europe for Businesses revolutionizing omnichannel payment acceptance, offering card issuing, loans, and deposit accounts to businesses across Europe. Licensed by the Bank of Greece, it operates under the EU FOS regime in 22 EU countries, delivering seamless financial services. Viva.com is the first to pioneer and holds the top position in the Tap on Any Device technology, enabling payments acceptance on any device or even without a device. Today Viva.com is leading the transition to All\-in\-One business solutions, powered by its full suite of financial services. To that end, reflecting its holistic approach to business needs, Viva.com has built a dynamic ecosystem featuring over 450 tech partner\-innovators in the software and hardware space. Connecting directly to all local payment schemes, alternative payment methods and payment systems across the continent, further strengthens Viva.com's value proposition. Viva.com envisions to provide businesses a value\-driven platform to accelerate the adoption of the latest technology. **Role Overview:** On\-site Representatives are responsible for promoting company's services remotely while providing merchants with the necessary support to ensure business continuity and optimal service usage. **Key Responsibilities:** * Promote company's services through remote interactions; * Assist merchants with verification and activation processes; * Monitor and analyze portfolio performance; * Provide remote support and consultation for existing customers; * Perform scheduled and self\-initiated activities; * Ensure new merchants in the assigned portfolio complete registration; * Enter and maintain up to date client information into the company's database and keep records with relevant data; * Analyze market trends and customer feedback to refine strategies; * Support existing merchants in maintaining active usage; * Escalate issues by opening cases for problems beyond their resolution scope; * Present product features and benefits to clients in a compelling manner. **Requirements** * 2\+ years of experience in business development within a fast\-paced environment preferably a financial services institution or similar SaaS solution; * Good knowledge of relevant computer programs (e.g. CRM software), social networking (LinkedIn) and telephone systems; * Excellent communication skills (verbal and written); * Excellent level of English; * Ability to work independently and under pressure; * A positive approach to objection handling; * Good organizational skills and attention to detail **Benefits** * Bonus, capped at an additional 18 months salary per annum * Private medical health insurance * 25 Days holiday \+ bank holidays * A diverse multi\-cultural working environment * Local office based in Dublin * 9am\-5:30pm working hours Monday to Friday

title: Clinical Services Psychologist  /Psychotherapist
description: **Aware Clinical Services Psychologist/Psychotherapist** **Job Overview:** We are seeking a compassionate, innovative, and enthusiastic psychologist or psychotherapist to join our friendly and dedicated team in the NGO sector. This is a new role offering an exciting opportunity to support the strategic clinical leadership across the range of services offered and shape high\-quality mental health service delivery for people living with depression, bipolar, and anxiety. **About Aware:** Aware is the national organisation providing free support, education, information and therapeutic services to people impacted by depression, bipolar and anxiety. We play a vital role in offering free mental health services across the country to the public. Aware works with a large team of mental health professionals and volunteers and training partners to deliver our wide range of vital services. **Our Vision** Aware undertakes to create a society where people affected by stress, depression, bipolar disorder and mood related conditions are understood, supported, free from stigma, and are encouraged to access appropriate therapies. **Our Values** Excellence, Compassion, Accountability and Integrity. **About the role** Reporting to Aware’s Clinical Director (Senior Clinical Psychologist), key aspects of the role include (but are not limited to): * To ensure the clinical effectiveness of Aware counselling and psychoeducation programmes by supporting clinical audit and other service development activities related to the services. * To contribute to creating a psychologically informed framework of service delivery across Aware Services * To provide clinical consultation/guidance and, where required, clinical supervision to Aware clinicians and service managers * To undertake direct assessment and triage with new service users to the Aware Counselling Service, in line with best practice. * To hold a small caseload, offering brief psychological therapy to adults presenting with mild to moderate depression and anxiety * To conduct risk assessments and implement risk management plans as appropriate * To contribute to research in line with the Aware Research Strategy and overall objectives * To plan and deliver teaching and training on workplace mental health workshops in line with best practice and current evidence base. * To represent Aware in public, policy, and sector\-wide mental health discussions. * To work with the communications department utilising the sector wide research / insights to inform our evidence\-informed communications strategy * Support the Clinical Director in offering external\-facing psychological leadership, specifically engaging state bodies and medical stakeholders to represent, promote, and advance Aware strategic objectives. Essential Skills and Experience: * Have an Irish post\-graduate professional psychology qualification (Doctorate or equivalent) accredited by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. Candidates qualified overseas must provide evidence of validation by the Department of Health at the time of application. Or * Psychotherapist fully accredited by the Irish Council for Psychotherapy (ICP) or the Irish Association for Counselling and Psychotherapy (IACP) * For psychologists, at least 5 years’ experience in professional psychology, with no less than 3 years spent in gaining the post graduate qualification and at least 1 year full time in post qualification experience as a professional psychologist. * For psychotherapists a minimum of five years post qualification/accreditation experience working in health and social care, therapeutic or related organisational settings supporting people with depression and anxiety * Commitment to evidence\-based practice and client\-centred care. * Effective presentation and facilitation skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner to a wide range of audiences * Excellent communication, teamwork, and interpersonal skills. * Comfortable with a wide range of IT systems. Desirable: * Experience of the not\-for\-profit sector * Experience of delivering and evaluating brief therapeutic interventions * Interest in service development and creation of psychoeducational and therapeutic resources in mental health Package * This is a permanent post. * Salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The salary range for this role will be €52,000 – €60,000 pro rata (0\.6 FTE / 22\.5 hours per week) * Aware is an equal opportunities employer. * The role is office based in 9 Upper Leeson Street, Dublin 4, offering a hybrid work model To apply please submit a one\-page cover letter outlining your suitability for the role and a CV (2 pages max) to info@aware.ie *before 5pm on 12th May* . Informal enquiries to Dr Susan Brannick on 01 661 7211\. Interviews will be held the week commencing 18th May.

title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist - Pectus Service
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist \- Pectus Service** **Department: Physiotherapy** **Reports to: Allied Therapy Manager** **Date: 2026** 10 hours per week **Overall Purpose of Job** We will provide exceptional patient care in an environment where quality, respect, caring and compassion are at the centre of all we do. **Key Responsibilities** **1\. PROFESSIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:** **1\.1\.1\.** Co\-ordinate and deliver an effective and efficient Pectus Physiotherapy Service, based on assessment of needs. **1\.1\.2\.** Develop and administer the highest quality of service by identifying and implementing operational procedures in line with best practice standards. **1\.1\.3\.** Keep abreast with new developments, skills and technology to ensure continuing competence in the job. **1\.1\.4\.** Enhance his/her skills in order to initiate and respond to changes in the work environment as additional roles may be demanded e.g. data analysis, clinical audit, continued professional development and research. **2\. MANAGEMENT RESPONSIBILITIES:** **2\.1\.1\.** To participate in the management of the Physiotherapy Service as a member of the Senior Physiotherapy Management team in co\-operation with the Allied Therapy Manager. **2\.1\.2\.** To create and promote good working relationships through open communication. **2\.1\.3\.** To contribute to and participate in the staff induction, staff appraisal and in\-service training programmes. **2\.1\.4\.** To actively participate in and promote continuing education and research activities consistent with the position. **2\.1\.5\.** To maintain appropriate records and statistics sufficient for organisational and legal purposes to include work related injuries/incidents. **2\.1\.6\.** Toregularly review and update Pectus Service Policies and Standard Operating Procedure documents. **2\.1\.7\.** To assist the Allied Therapy Manager in achieving Quality and Accreditation requirements for Physiotherapy. **2\.1\.8\.** To provide in intra disciplinary education to promote physiotherapy service in Beacon Hospital. **2\.1\.9\.** To provide flexible assistance to the Allied Therapy Manager in delivering clinical care and in operational roles such as Policy and Procedure Document updates, Beacon Hospital committee participation where required. **3\. STAFF MANAGEMENT** **3\.1\.1\.** To lead by example a professional, dedicated and punctual team. **3\.1\.2\.** To encourage professional development of team members through promotion of self\-learning, formal courses and in\-service training. **NOTE 1\.** The hours of work are at the discretion of the Physiotherapy Manager and in line with service needs. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Candidates must hold a degree in Physiotherapy, be registered with CORU and membership of the Irish Society of Chartered Physiotherapists is desirable. **Experience** (Amount and Type) Candidates must hold at least 5 years post graduate experience in the area of Orthopaedic or Musculoskeletal physiotherapy. A suitable postgraduate qualification in Orthopaedics, Musculoskeletal practice is desirable. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** In addition to the responsibilities and core competencies of a senior physiotherapist: ***The candidate must have/display the following attributes:*** Applies expert clinical knowledge and reasoning skills in assessment, planning, Implementation and evaluation of patient interventions Carries out clinical skills in the area in keeping with current evidence\-based practice Delivers a holistic patient focused approach and embraces the MDT perspective Applies evidence\-based outcome measures to evaluate efficacy of treatment and Quality of care Balances clinical demands with other responsibilities. * Demonstrates an educational role for example as a 'mentor. * Participates in research. * Generates new knowledge and skills in the specified area of Physiotherapy. * Disseminates advanced ethical awareness and responsibilities in specific areas. * Demonstrates leadership and an understanding of service planning. Demonstrates a critical understanding of the context in which practice occurs for example social, political and inter professional collaboration. Strong evidence of Continuing Professional Development \- Strength and Conditioning etc. is desirable **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager of hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: Cloud DevOps Engineer
description: What you are expected to have: * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field with 10\-15 years of overall IT experience. * Minimum of 5\-10 years of relevant experience in cloud engineering and DevOps. * In\-depth knowledge and hands\-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. * Proficient in infrastructure\-as\-code (IaC) tools, such as Terraform or CloudFormation or ARM. * Solid understanding of containerization technologies, particularly Docker and Kubernetes especially EKS, ECS and AKS. * Experience in implementing and managing CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitLab CI, Bamboo or CircleCI. * Strong scripting skills in languages like Python, Shell, or PowerShell. * Familiarity with configuration management tools such as Ansible or Puppet. * Knowledge of networking principles and security best practices in cloud environments. * Deploying AI solutions on AWS using AWS Bedrock, Sagemaker etc. * Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (Preferred). * Relevant certifications in cloud platforms and DevOps tools (Preferred). * Experience in an IT consulting services environment (Preferred). * Advanced proficiency in cloud\-native services and serverless architectures (Preferred). * Knowledge of monitoring and logging tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, or Splunk (Preferred). * Expertise in optimizing cloud costs and resource management (Preferred). * Strong communication skills with the ability to convey technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders (Preferred). * Active participation in the open\-source community or contributions to relevant projects (Preferred). You are will do: * Collaborate with clients and internal teams to understand and analyze their technical requirements. * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure solutions with a focus on scalability, security, and performance. * Implement and optimize CI/CD pipelines using DevOps best practices. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to cloud infrastructure and deployments. * Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies in cloud computing and DevOps. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure successful delivery of cloud\-based projects. * Provide technical support and guidance to junior team members.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)
description: **Cloud Solutions Architect** **Daily Rate Contract (1 day onsite)** My Dublin based client is seeking an experienced Cloud Solutions Architect for a daily rate contract. The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * Ensure solutions meet performance, reliability, and governance standards **Required Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience designing and implementing scalable cloud solutions * Strong background in modern cloud\-native architecture (microservices, serverless, containerisation) * Hands\-on Infrastructure as Code expertise (Terraform, CloudFormation) * Deep experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS or Azure) * Experience designing or implementing Kubernetes solutions (preferably EKS or equivalent) * Proven ability to manage and optimise public cloud infrastructure environments * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Experience producing clear, structured architecture and design documentation **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to regulated or complex enterprise environments * Experience across both AWS and Azure * CI/CD and DevOps implementation using IaC tooling * Experience supporting cloud migration or transformation programmes **Certifications (Advantageous)** * AWS Certified Solutions Architect (or equivalent cloud certification)

title: Cloud Support Engineer
description: **Job Description:** Our client, a leading financial services technology organisation, is seeking a Senior Cloud Support Engineer to join their service delivery function, supporting and maintaining a global cloud\-based platform. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide high\-quality technical support to end users, ensuring service levels and customer satisfaction targets are consistently achieved * Deploy, manage and optimise cloud\-based infrastructure to ensure performance, scalability and security across production environments * Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using modern tooling to support efficient software delivery * Monitor system performance, availability and capacity, proactively addressing issues and improving resilience * Manage system upgrades, patching activities and configuration changes across environments * Act as an escalation point for complex technical issues and drive timely resolution * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver reliable and scalable technical solutions * Develop and maintain infrastructure as code using tools such as Terraform * Implement monitoring, logging and observability practices across platforms * Support incident, problem and change management processes aligned to ITIL frameworks * Drive continuous improvement through automation, optimisation and operational best practices **Experience Required** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in cloud infrastructure, technical operations or a similar role * Proven experience supporting highly available, 24/7 cloud\-based environments * Strong hands\-on experience with cloud platforms, particularly Microsoft Azure, alongside exposure to AWS * Experience with cloud\-native architectures and distributed systems * Demonstrated experience in customer\-facing support roles within technical environments * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills across infrastructure and application layers * Experience with monitoring and alerting tools within cloud environments * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment tooling such as Azure DevOps * Experience with identity and access management principles, including role\-based access controls * Proven ability to manage incidents effectively and maintain composure in high\-pressure situations **Desirable Skills** * Experience with platform security best practices and secure\-by\-design principles * Knowledge of vulnerability management and remediation approaches * Exposure to infrastructure automation and configuration management tools * Experience implementing observability frameworks including logging and metrics collection * Understanding of modern DevOps practices and continuous delivery models * Strong communication skills with the ability to work across global teams **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Opportunity to work within a global, technology\-driven environment * Exposure to modern cloud\-native technologies and large\-scale platforms * Collaborative and supportive team culture with opportunities for professional growth **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **Similar Roles Open** As a specialised recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Computer Numerical Control Machinist
description: 🕒 **Employment Type:** Full\-time, Permanent (Shift Work) Our client, a leading precision engineering manufacturer, is seeking a Machine Operative – CNC Machining to join their high\-performing production team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a long\-term career in advanced manufacturing, working with modern CNC equipment in a clean, organised, and quality\-driven environment. Full training and development will be provided, with clear progression pathways for candidates who demonstrate initiative, reliability, and strong attention to detail. **Responsibilities include but are not limited to:** * Select, load, and run CNC programs in line with job specifications. * Set cutting tools, speeds, and feeds according to job packages and engineering drawings. * Achieve target cycle times and support overall production output goals. * Carry out quality inspections and measurements to ensure components meet required standards. * Perform routine machine and tooling maintenance in line with preventive maintenance plans. * Troubleshoot basic machine and process issues to minimise downtime. * Manage tooling and materials, including reordering as required. * Participate in continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency and quality. * Maintain a clean and organised work area in line with 5S principles. * Communicate any issues or deviations to supervisors and quality teams promptly. * Adhere to all health and safety procedures and maintain a safe working environment. * Carry out additional duties as required to support production operations. **Job Requirements:** * Previous experience in a manufacturing environment, ideally in CNC machining or turning operations. * Experience working shifts in a production environment is desirable. * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings. * Strong mechanical aptitude and problem\-solving skills. * Good level of numeracy and attention to detail. * Basic computer skills and familiarity with production systems. * Strong communication skills and ability to work as part of a team. **What’s on Offer:** * Full training and structured development pathways * Opportunity to progress within a high\-tech manufacturing environment * Exposure to modern CNC machinery and precision engineering processes * Competitive salary and shift allowance (where applicable) * Stable, long\-term career opportunities *Immediate interviews available for suitable candidates.* *Note: By applying for this position, you may also be considered by Pale Blue Dot® Recruitment for other relevant opportunities.* **Pale Blue Dot® Recruitment \- The Experts in STEM Workforce Solutions**

title: Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship
description: locations Navan time type Full time posted on Posted Today time left to apply End Date: June 20, 2026 (30\+ days left to apply) job requisition id JOB\_015069**Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship – Boliden Tara Mines** **Build a skilled, future\-ready career with support, purpose and real opportunities to grow.** At Boliden Tara Mines, we provide the metals essential to improve society for generations to come. We are guided by our values of Care, Courage and Responsibility, and by our ambition to be the most climate\-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. If you enjoy problem solving, technology, and learning how complex systems work \- this apprenticeship could be for you. **Thinking about your next step ?** A **Construction Plant Fitting Apprenticeship** at Tara Mines offers a practical, structured way to **earn while you learn**, gain a nationally recognised qualification, and build skills that are in demand across many industries. On successful completion, apprentices will achieve a **QQI Advanced Certificate – Level 6**, a nationally recognised qualification valued across multiple industries. You don’t need previous experience \- we provide training, mentoring and support from day one. **What you’ll learn and do** **Construction Plant Fitters** support with the maintenance and reliability of mobile plant and equipment used across our mining operations. With guidance from experienced mentors, you’ll learn how these machines operate and why safety, quality and teamwork are critical in everything we do. During your apprenticeship, you’ll be supported to learn how to: * Diagnose faults and carry out repairs on mobile plant and equipment * Perform routine servicing and preventative maintenance to keep equipment operating safely and efficiently * Complete inspections and safety checks in line with mining regulations and site standards * Use modern maintenance systems and technology, including the CMMS system (Maximo) * Develop strong problem‑solving skills through proactive fault‑finding and repair activities **Your support and development** Boliden Tara Mines offers world‑class technical training, along with competitive pay and excellent rewards as you progress and develop your skills. You will be part of a safety\-focused, inclusive team, supported by experienced and qualified craftspeople who will: * Coach and guide you throughout your apprenticeship * Help you build confidence in your technical skills * Encourage questions and continuous learning We take pride in creating an environment where apprentices feel supported, respected and safe to learn. **Entry requirements** * Aged 18 years or over * Leaving Certificate with a minimum of five passes at Ordinary Level, including English and Maths **Skills and qualities we look for** * Curiosity and interest in how things work * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and ask questions * A positive, proactive approach * An interest in technology, engineering or problem solving **Practical details** * 39 hours per week, Monday to Friday * Some flexibility required in line with operational needs * A structured apprenticeship combining on\-the\-job learning with off\-site training **Opportunities within the wider Boliden Group** An apprenticeship with Boliden Tara Mines offers more than a qualification — it’s the start of a long‑term career. Graduates can grow onsite and across the **wider Boliden Group**, with skills valued nationally and internationally. **Inclusion matters to us** At Boliden Tara Mines, we are committed to a workplace where women and men feel welcome, supported and confident to succeed. We encourage applications from all backgrounds, including people who may not have previously considered a career in Electrical Instrumentation If you’re interested but unsure whether you meet all the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. If this career path interests you, we welcome your application. **Closing date: 19 June 2026** **If this opportunity interests you, we welcome your application via Boliden Careers.** \- Important! If you are a current employee within Boliden you shouldn't complete the application at this site, please log on to your Workday account

title: Corporate Services Assistant
description: **Date:** 1 May 2026 **Location:** Dublin, IE, D01 W213 **Company:** Walkers Global **Overview of Walkers** ----------------------- Walkers is an international firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to the global financial services community. Walkers Professional Services ("WPS") sets a “best in class” standard in the provision of corporate services to aircraft leasing and structured finance special purpose vehicles in Ireland. WPS is led by a team of over 50 highly skilled professionals drawn from accounting, audit, company secretarial, compliance and legal fields. The team combines a depth of specialised experience with a focus on consistently delivering high quality results to clients at all times. At WPS, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone can feel comfortable, happy and confident to be themselves. We believe that a diverse and inclusive business is more collaborative and innovative and provides great opportunity for our staff which in turn, allows us to better serve the needs of our clients. Additionally, we actively encourage participation in internal committees such as Corporate \& Social Responsibility and Diversity and Inclusion along with other firm wide initiatives. Walkers is an equal opportunities employer. **The Role** ------------ WPS is currently seeking a Corporate Services Assistant to join our dynamic and growing transaction team in Dublin. In this role, you will be part of a busy, client facing, professionally qualified team. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. Please note, this is a fully onsite role and applicants must be willing and able to work five days per week in our Dublin office. **Duties and Responsibilities** ------------------------------- You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Assisting senior transaction executives and directors in the management of a portfolio of SPVs involved in structured finance and aircraft leasing; * Building and maintaining strong client relationships, ensuring a high quality, responsive and professional service to clients at all times; * Leading the process of on\-boarding new clients; * Assisting with a variety of company secretarial duties including coordinating board meetings, company incorporations, annual compliance, filings with the Companies Registration Office and other ad hoc duties; * Establishing and maintaining statutory books and records; * Assisting in the incorporation and setting up of new entities; * Attending board meetings and drafting non\-transactional board minutes; * Liaising with investment managers, arrangers, lessors, lawyers and other relevant parties; * Assisting with bank account openings, FATCA registrations, EMIR obligations and other necessary SPV transaction management tasks as required; * Contributing to internal projects and initiatives arising from changes in statutory or regulatory obligations; mutually suitable repayment plans. * Liaising with billing and other relevant departments to resolve outstanding customer issues that are creating a barrier to payment. * Working with third\-party collections agencies and dealing with exceptions on final overdue accounts. * Ensuring compliance with company procedures and regulatory policies when it comes to the removal of energy supply. * Working with our onboarding teams to assess the credit risk of new customers coming on to supply. * Adhoc duties as required. **Skills, Knowledge And Expertise** * Minimum 2 years’ experience in a Management system beneficial. * Excel skills and good working knowledge of MS Office would be an advantage. **Benefits** * Competitive salary and bonus structure * Private Pension Contribution Scheme Exclusive PrepayPower Staff Tariff * Modern offices with excellent facilities in Sandyford and flexible hybrid working options * Active CSR, Wellness, and Social Committees plus great social events * Refer\-a\-Friend Scheme * TaxSaver Travel Tickets \& Bike\-to\-Work Scheme **About Yuno Energy** Since entering the Irish market in late 2023, we’ve been transforming the residential energy sector We are redefining how people understand and engage with their energy. Our innovative approach delivers radical transparency and rewards energy efficiency. With just a few taps, customers can gain a clear view of their energy habits—tracking every hour, unit, and cent of their usage. In 2025, together with PrepayPower, we proudly reached over 350,000 customer connections. **Why Work at Yuno Energy** At Yuno Energy, we believe work should be both challenging and rewarding. Ranked among Ireland’s Great Places to Work *,* we offer ambitious individuals a dynamic, fast\-paced environment with exciting opportunities for growth. title: Data Analyst
description: **Company Overview** Holafly is a high\-growth scale\-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500\+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity. We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them. **About The Role** We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply. **Responsibilities** * Collect, clean, analyse, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. * Create clear, concise, and impactful visualisations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders. * Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools. * Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision\-making. * Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimisation. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualisation. **Qualifications** * Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. * Strong proficiency in SQL. * Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Looker Studio, Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik Sense). * Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and critical thinking abilities. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity. * Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline. **Hybrid Modality** We focus on trust and performance. We are implementing a Two phased approach for our Hybrid Modality: Phase 1: While the teams are establishing ways of working, building trust, and developing their rhythm, we ask that you’re present in the office Three days a week. In\-person proximity is key to accelerating the forming stage. Phase 2: Once the teams are performing, we move to a more relaxed hybrid model. We ask for a minimum of one day per week in the office, centred around critical ceremonies such as sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. **Benefits \& Perks** * 25 Days of PTO * 10% pension contribution * € 3000 for Medical/Health coverage * Self development budget of $ 500 During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection. Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country? Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do. We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

As a data center O \& M personnel, you will have the following responsibilities: * Develop standardized operation management procedures covering Facilities, IT, Assets, and Security for Alibaba Cloud’s large\-scale IDC data centers. * Explore and apply advanced O\&M technologies in digitalization, automation, and intelligence. * Oversee execution of operation standards for incidents, issues, changes, risks, maintenance, and personnel to achieve performance targets. * Drive internal and external communication and collaboration to optimize resource utilization, align with business goals, and meet customer SLA requirements. * Ensure the safety, stability, and efficiency of data center operations. * Contribute to R\&D, planning, solution design, construction, and delivery of IDC infrastructure (energy, buildings, power, HVAC, monitoring, networks, servers, etc.). * Stay current with technological trends and support the evolution of data center architecture to strengthen Alibaba Cloud IDC’s competitiveness. **Job Requirements** * Solid knowledge of computer software, hardware, and IT fundamentals; understanding of foundational IT network architecture; ability in data analysis and mining. * Strong learning ability, curiosity about emerging technologies, and adaptability to new environments. * Excellent communication, teamwork, and independent problem\-solving skills. * Fluent in Chinese and English (listening, speaking, reading, and writing) as a working language, or proficiency in other local languages. * Internship or project experience in cloud computing or the data center industry is preferred.

title: Data Engineer, Data Architecture and Engineering
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience in data engineering, database querying (e.g., SQL), developing data pipelines (ETL) and data visualization (dashboards/reports). * 3 years of experience coding in one or more programming languages. * 3 years of experience working with data infrastructure and data models by performing exploratory queries and scripts. * 3 years of experience designing data pipelines, and dimensional data modeling for synch and asynch system integration and implementation using internal (e.g., Flume, etc.) and external stacks (DataFlow, Spark, etc.). ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience partnering with stakeholders (e.g., users, partners, customer), and managing stakeholders/customers. * 3 years of experience developing project plans and delivering projects on time within budget and scope. * Experience using AI technologies to augment, improve or automate the development process. * Experience writing and maintaining ETLs which operate on a variety of structured and unstructured sources. * Experience in large\-scale distributed data processing, including familiarity with NoSQL databases. **About the job** ----------------- The gSO Data, Architecture, Tools and Analytics (gData) team empowers Google to make brilliant business decisions by delivering critical data infrastructure and actionable insights. Supporting the global gTech Ads organization, gData manages massive datasets to solve complex, non\-routine investigative issues. Ultimately, our insights optimize operations and enhance the advertiser experience that drives the majority of Alphabet's business. As a Data Engineer in gData, you will design and build the foundational data infrastructure that powers the global gTech Ads organization. You’ll address complex, large\-scale issues using Google’s proprietary tech stack to deliver a reliable single source of truth. In this role, you will have the opportunity to architect innovative data pipelines and solutions that directly enable predictive analytics and AI\-driven business decisions at scale. Google creates products and services that make the world a better place, and gTech’s role is to help bring them to life. Our teams of trusted advisors support customers globally. Our solutions are rooted in our technical skill, product expertise, and a thorough understanding of our customers’ complex needs. Whether the answer is a bespoke solution to solve a unique problem, or a new tool that can scale across Google, everything we do aims to ensure our customers benefit from the full potential of Google products. To learn more about gTech, check out our video. **Responsibilities** -------------------- * Design, develop, test, and maintain reliable and scalable data pipelines and Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL)/Extract, Load, and Transform (ELT) architectures using Google's distributed data systems (e.g., advanced SQL, Python). * Contribute to the development of our data infrastructure and data platform, optimizing data models to ensure our single source of truth remains performant. * Partner closely with cross\-functional stakeholders across business and engineering teams to translate evolving analytics requirements into actionable technical data solutions. * Work seamlessly with data scientists and business analysts to transition investigative prototypes, metrics, and models into stable, production\-grade reporting environments. * Advocate data quality by authoring clear technical design documents, executing code reviews, and proactively resolving complex bugs and supporting escalations. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.
